name: SlashSlack
description: An easy-to-run Slack clone that lives on your own server. Channels, DMs, file uploads and invites — your team chat, self-hosted.

inputs.label: Session secret
inputs.help: Long random secret used to encrypt session cookies. Generated automatically.

compose:
name: "slashnode-slashslack"
services:
  slashslack:
    image: "ghcr.io/slashbinslashnoname/slashslack:latest"
    container_name: "slashslack"
    restart: "unless-stopped"
    networks:
      - slashnode
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    environment:
      PORT: "3000"
      NODE_ENV: "production"
      SESSION_SECRET: "${secret.SESSION_SECRET}"
      DATA_DIR: "/app/data"
      UPLOAD_DIR: "/app/uploads"
      PUBLIC_ORIGIN: "https://slashslack.${node.exports.host}"
    volumes:
      - "slashslack-data:/app/data"
      - "slashslack-uploads:/app/uploads"
networks:
  slashnode:
    external: true
volumes:
  slashslack-data:
    name: "slashnode-slashslack_slashslack_slashslack-data"
  slashslack-uploads:
    name: "slashnode-slashslack_slashslack_slashslack-uploads"
notes: The image is published to GitHub Container Registry. Make the package public (repo → Packages → Package settings → Visibility) or the image pull will fail.
